{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 736 ensures that adjunct faculty are compensated fairly and treated as exempt professionals.",
    "AB 736 establishes a minimum compensation floor for adjunct faculty.",
    "The bill represents a substantial pay increase for many faculty members.",
    "Adjunct faculty have historically been paid on a per-course or per-unit basis.",
    "Without AB 736, colleges could have been forced to convert adjunct faculty to hourly, non-exempt employees.",
    "Lawsuits over ambiguities in current law are redirecting institutional funds away from academic services and programming.",
    "AB 736 will create a minimum pay rate for adjunct faculty that will rise with inflation.",
    "The raise for the lowest-paid adjuncts in the state could be as much as 50%.",
    "The provisions of AB 736 became law immediately upon the Governor's signature."
  ]
